title: 2024 Lee County Employers & Population Information
content:
As many of you know Olympia Marketing is based in Estero Florida - which is inside of Lee County. We're proud to be in this area and have been here for a considerable time. Some might call it a LONG time! We've seen the area grow from nothing more than cow pastures, to a budding metropolis.

This last year Olympia has been honored to work with the Engage Estero community organization and is working towards a number of major initiatives for Estero & Lee County. We'll be debuting those projects when the time is right - and we're very excited to be giving back to the community that gave birth to us - however, this blog post is a summation of some of the interesting findings we've learned about Estero & Lee County along the way as well as some of our more concerning thoughts and obvservations about the economy generally, and specifically to the area.

Brief History of Estero & Lee County

Thank you to Engage Estero for putting together this page and content around the history of Estero housing. Back in 1997

the Estero Fire District conducted a survey of all structures in Estero and found about 4,600 housing units of all types, many of them mobile homes. The building boom started in 1998, as the airport, Florida Gulf Coast University and Miromar Outlets began attracting residential development to the area. There were 550 housing units built in 1998; the following year, 1999, that number more than doubled to 1,150. By the end of the 20th century, the total number of housing units in Estero was about 6,300.

Today Estero has 38,000 active residents with another 20,000+ units under construction out in East Lee County. A total of explosion of 10x in the last 25 years, and another planned doubling in the next 5 years (if those units can be sold).

Lee County more broadly has seen similar (albeit less pronounced) growth in the same time period.

So let's get to the meat of this blog post.

Employers in Lee County

As a part of our recent project we were tasked with doing some research on the local businesses in the region and the likelihood a particular project would get funding (supported through non-tax deductible donations). We found a few interesting pieces of information and resources. FGCU has kept a cool running list of the top 100 employers in the region for sometime. If one sorts for only lee county this is what you find:

Notable Highlights

Lee Health - a non-profit employs almost 10x the employees as FGCU (this is going to be important when we come back to some things). It's fascinating they've remained a non-profit and that lee county's largest employer is a non-profit I had never realized this having lived and worked here for 30+ years. Also, the size and power healthcare systems like this have in local communities is unbelievable (especially since the COVID grift). As someone that's studied health, wellness, fitness, nutrition, at both the mainstream and the more fringe level - these systems are complete criminals in how they operate. On the other hand, with our retirement focus as a region - this does make sense (at least economically).

Government - Roughly 20% of the top 25 employers are government and they make up the largest single sector of employers/employees for all of lee county - I suspect this is the case for ALL organizations with the exception of sole proprietors or VERY small businesses (IE individual consultants, people that run a business with just one or two employees besides themselves, etc.).

70,000 Totally Employed - The total of the top 25 employers is roughly 70,000. With an employed population of 300,000+ in lee county, this means roughly a quarter of a million people are employed by companies outside this list, in other areas - or small local businesses.

Little Outside Dollars - With a few exceptions (which I'll document below) even in the top 25 companies, there are very few that bring in outside dollars to the community. Based on my own quick assumptions it looks like only 6 (or 24% of the companies or only 16% of the jobs) concentrate in anyway on bringing outside dollars into the region, to local companies. Instead, most of the industry here is to cannabalize our existing retiree base (IE primarily a low-income service economy that maintains light entertainment, services, restaurants, etc.).

Interesting Local Highlights:

Chicos - a locally operated public firm is a great example of (although its clearly a dying a company) the kinds of companies we need in the region who bring outside wealth into the region.

Hertz & Herc - Similar to the above. But, bringing Hertz was clearly a disaster for Lee County in terms of return on investment. The Hertz building is currently 90% unoccupied, hertz has (and will even more) struggle. Herc on the other hand seems like a great business and had no idea more people are employed there than in Hertz proper.

Gartner - Similar to Chicos in terms of outside production. We should be working to attract more large businesses like this to the region if we're going to transform (which is the next section).

Southwest Florida's History

Southwest Florida has been a hodgepodge of cheap land and lack of vision or will from a developmental stand point. The region has grown steadily in part due to large macro-factors in the economy relating to housing (IE the huge housing boom of the mid 2000s). Covid, similarly spurned massive growth - in part because the region was EXCEPTIONALLY AGAINST LOCKDOWN MEASURES (as everyone should have been). I'd estimate we have one of the highest unvaccinated rates in the country as well - which is going to bode well for our future.

With that said, there's never been a clear growth plan. Many have been talking for 20+ years at the county government level about the real issues with the regions and what shifting demographics will mean. Many at the local Chamber & EDC level have basically said... "it sure would be great if we had more... tech companies." The "greatest generation" who really settled most of Florida - especially these retirement communities that litter Southwest Florida, have virtually all died out. And the Boomers have taken up the mantle of continuing to uphold Florida as the United States' pre-eminent retirement destination.

However, less well known is that Florida actually doesn't have many hospice or end of life care facilities when it comes to other states. In fact FLORIDA ISN'T EVEN IN THE TOP 10 in END OF LIFECARE/HOSPICE facilities. Here's a great massive research piece on the top states for hospice, etc.

Top 10 Hospice/End of Life Care Facility States:

  1. Oregon
  2. Hawaii
  3. Washington
  4. Vermont
  5. Montana
  6. Maine
  7. New Mexico
  8. Rhode Island
  9. Idaho
  10. Utah

South(West) Florida's Massive Problem(s)

For 4 generations now South Florida in particular has been considered a retirement community. If Miami spun that into (through its international connections, the atlantic, large ports, and widespread fraud) a larger vision - South WEST Florida - has continued to quietly cling to this even as other areas around it grow (we often pretend this isn't the case, but it's still the only thing supporting this community). Here's a list of the major problems I see with southwest Florida and why that may spell disaster in the next few years:

  1. No Central Industry - As the detailed information above highlights, there is no central industry or industries in the region keeping this area afloat - outside of the existing wealth that was created in other places and moved here on the collection of ideas of ("florida cheap and free - and this is a nice area").
  2. #1 Industry is DEATH CARE (what I like to call 'healthcare' systems - as they don't actually create health, but continually facilitate virtually no quality of life at the expense of all assets a person or family has - staving off death).
  3. Primarily Wealth Boomers - Other regions (take Minneapolis for example) have large companies en mass that bring in revenue from activities all over the country or world. Southwest Florida, as we've proven has no such revenue generating activities.
  4. Very Little Natural Exploitation - Lee County in particular does a poor job exploiting its natural resources and highlighting/protecting them and projecting them as major growth factors. Sure, this is done in more subtle ways through the promotion of restaurants and hotels.
  5. Beaches & The Water - Similarly here one of the main draws of the region (water and the beach) has all but been eliminated as there have ONLY ever been two major accesses (one of which is controlled by the other) - Bonita Beach & Fort Myers Beach. Bonita (particularly Bonita Beach) should be a beautiful beach town easily demonstrable from 75 West to the beach. Instead its a patchwork of retarded ideas, with a very ugly corridor and now, even in Summer (lowest traffic) it's virtually impossible even for RESIDENTS to get to the beach. The county has what - 100 parking spots? For a region that has a million residents+ and in season triples in size?
  6. NO Infrastructure - When this place was sold originally the land was primarily sold off to low-income speculators. There was no plan to ever have functioning cities. A great resource on this is Swamp Peddlers which talks about the history, politics, and issues we see today. It's why areas like Lehigh - will never have proper infrastructure and will have to be saved by the residents themselves. There are still areas in the region where there are no property records.
  7. No Ports - Because of the shallow gulf there are no deep ports facilitating delivery or shipping of any goods from a maritime perspective. This will always inhibit
  8. Roads - There is literally only ONE road - 75 for any kind of actual good delivery from the other side of the state.

The Future

In my next post on this continuing research project I'll go through the future and what are some things I see we can actively work towards, due, or attract as a region as we head into the second generation of the new millenium.
seo_title: Lee County 2024: Employers & Population Info | SEO Data
seo_description: Explore Lee County's 2024 job market and population data. Olympia Marketing offers key insights for businesses and job seekers in Estero, FL.

title: How Local PR Helps Drive Links for SEO: A Case Study on Skincare Academy of Florida
content:
Want to grow your local rankings? Want to gain press for your business? Want more business not just one-time, but permanently? Then read on... we'll show you how local PR effects local SEO efforts, why Local PR is necessary, the basics of local search engine optimization, and some explicit examples of how this has worked with clients in the past.

How Do You Grow SEO? Get Local Links? PR Is a Fantastic Way!

Having performed digital marketing for over 20 years now I constantly get asked (by sophisticated clients) "how can my website perform better?" Many businesses take a little bit of coaching to understand that YOUR AUDIENCE is out there... SEARCHING FOR YOUR BUSINESS. The days of drumming up business have long passed (unless you're in a new market, or with something truly unique). Now-a-days however, MOST BUSINESSES DON'T REALIZE THERE ARE LITERALLY THOUSANDS OF PEOPLE IN YOUR AREA SEARCHING FOR YOUR BUSINESS, PRODUCTS AND SERVICES. Yes... right now. That's the topic for another post entirely (one we're working on a video for).

But this post is about those businesses that have taken it upon themselves to understand the web and they completely want to dominate. Do you want to get more business? Are you looking for tips to increase your local SEO or ranking for particular searches? Well... keep reading, because this blog post is chock full of nuggets.

Why Local Search Engine Optimization (SEO) is SO IMPORTANT

Before I get to the meat of this blog post about Local PR and it's impact on search engine optimization (mostly local SEO) I want to ask you something...

What if there was a way for you to accomplish all of the following?...

  • Build trust with your prospective customers
  • Build massive brand awareness and trust
  • Increase the number of leads your website receives almost immediately (with the benefits only INCREASING over time)
  • And MUCH MORE...
  • All at a DISCOUNT to conventional advertising?

It sounds great right? Well we're going to dive into all of that right now with this guide on Local PR and how it affects your website's search engine optimization and local SEO.

How PR Affects Local Search Rankings

Public Relations (PR) plays an instrumental role in how we perform search engine optimization (most notably local SEO) at Olympia Marketing. Local PR is a strategic tool that not only enhances visibility but also drives valuable links to a website - which in turn drives rankings - which in turn drives revenue. In this blog post, we'll delve into a real-world example from Olympia Marketing, a digital-first marketing firm, to illustrate the profound impact of local PR on SEO. We'll focus on a case study involving the Skincare Academy of Florida, a Bonita Springs-based esthetician and massage school, to demonstrate how local PR can drive links and improve search engine rankings.

The Power of Local SEO and PR - Quick Overview of Local SEO

Local SEO focuses on optimizing a website to attract traffic from location-based searches. For small businesses, mastering local SEO is crucial for gaining visibility in you community. One powerful tactic within local SEO is leveraging PR to secure mentions and links from local news outlets, which can significantly enhance a business's online presence.

How PR Works Alongside Local SEO (Search Engine Optimization) Works

Let's go back over the points we mentioned above in terms of the large benefits of PR & Local SEO...

  • Build trust with your prospective customers
  • Build massive brand awareness and trust
  • Increase the number of leads your website receives almost immediately (with the benefits only INCREASING over time)
  • And MUCH more...
  • All at a DISCOUNT to conventional advertising?

Let's address these one-by-one.

Build Trust - Earned media, as PR is often referred to - is media that has been earned by your company or organization, you ARE NOT paying directly for advertising. Instead our team gets to know local/national outlets, along with their publishing schedule/calendar. We understand your business, and the unique things happening and draft a press release about select topics we believe will be picked up. We then solicit publications - getting them to publish the press release and often even more press (about ancillary topics). Here's the best part - when readers KNOW they're looking at an ad, they often treat it far differently. Earned media, on the other hand has the huge added benefit of not just being an "advertisement" for your company, but it's one positioned with the authority of the news publication behind it - a massive endorsement. And this endorsement doesn't just have a positive effect on human readers - it also has a huge effect on Google (and other search engines), but we'll get back to that.

Build Brand Awareness - There's no faster way to build brand awareness than by leveraging the audience and size of publications. In the case study below - for example, roughly 100,000 people in a small Southwest Florida region were able to learn about our client overnight - something that would require far more time or effort if done differently.

Increase the Number of Leads - Ok, to understand this we have to take a step back to understand SEO. Long story short though - by being mentioned in these publications you'll both receive a LINK from many of these publications (which is like a vote of confidence in the eyes of the search engines) but you'll also gain notoriety in Google's Knowledge Graph system around the topics discussed in your media piece. Both have a cumulative effect on your website's "power" in the search engines - causing you to rank higher and gain more business - often almost overnight. In the example below 3 links from local publications led to a boost in rankings to #1 for relevant searches LITERALLY OVERNIGHT.

Discount - Finally, if you were to PAY for advertising on the same scale as earned media it would literally be 2x-3x MORE to JUST BUY the ads... (and as mentioned above it doesn't come with the same implicit weight as articles written by the publication directly).

Google Knowledge Graph

Let's take a quick step back before we dive into the case study let's take a brief moment to explain one of the behemoths of Google's search algorithim, that rarely gets mentioned these days - Google's Knowledge Graph. As the above image illustrates Google's Knowledge Graph consists of a number of components represented in the above "Knowledge Panels." You've probably seen these panels before if you've ever searched for a business, a movie or tv show, a celebrity, or other "entity." These tools provide quick, factual summaries about people, places, and things directly in search results.

The Knowledge Graph aggregates information from various sources, including open databases and licensed content, to present over 500 billion facts about five billion entities. It automatically generates panels, but users can report inaccuracies or suggest changes. On mobile, panels are integrated into search results to fit the screen size. Google emphasizes accuracy and offers ways for entities to claim their panels and update details.

Why does this concern you? Well - by performing press releases Google takes what it ALREADY knows about your business - and builds an even deeper profile on your company based on what is written in your press articles. This, individually, and over-time, can lead to a massive increase in your search presence or search rankings.

Now with all of this said, let's address a recent client scenario in which we did local PR strategically in order to help a client grow their local Search Engine Optimization.

Bonita Springs Esthetics School PR & Local SEO Case Study

In this case study, we'll explore how local PR efforts for the Skincare Academy of Florida - a longtime client and Bonita Springs Based Esthetics School - resulted in substantial SEO benefits, including improved rankings and increased traffic.

Setting the Stage: Skincare Academy's Growth and PR Strategy

First a background on the discussions that took place... The Skincare Academy of Florida has recently launched two new initiatives: an esthetician school and a massage school. To promote these new offerings, we implemented a multi-faceted PR strategy, focusing on both local and national levels. For local businesses like Skincare Academy, local PR is essential for targeting relevant audiences and obtaining valuable local backlinks.

One key component of our strategy was issuing a press release to announce the launch of the new massage school. This press release was distributed to various local news outlets, aiming to generate buzz and drive links back to the Skincare Academy's website.

Analyzing the Impact: Tracking SEO Improvements

To measure the impact of our local PR efforts, we closely monitored the Skincare Academy's search engine rankings, website traffic as well as leads - through a number of systems (semrush, analytics, webmaster tools/search console and finally through their website form tracking software).

The results have been impressive.

Within a week of the press release, we observed a significant boost in rankings for relevant keywords such as "Bonita Springs massage school" and "Naples beauty school."

Using tools like Google Search Console and SEMrush, we tracked keyword positions, impressions, and clicks. The data revealed that the Skincare Academy experienced substantial improvements in organic search rankings, thanks to the backlinks obtained through local PR.

Within approximately 1 month (although we were monitoring day-to-day to create this analysis) we saw an increase in organic traffic of approximately 13%+ or around 300 new users. Based on our current contact form submission rate and our total value/lead that means THIS SINGLE PRESS RELEASE which was approximately $1500 brought in $270,000 in potential income. In fact, based on our tracking and analysis we were able to unseat a long time competitor and steal around a half-dozen customers. And this is ONLY THE FIRST MONTH. These trends will continue to move the organic rankings of the site up over time, and if we're able to implement bi-monthly press releases as planned, we're going to help our client completely corner the digital market - at which time we'll work with them to spread geographically, as well as offering online services, and finally implement a more conventional marketing, advertising and branding approach to stir up the most business.

The Long-Term Benefits of Local PR

Local PR is not just a short-term tactic; it offers long-lasting benefits for SEO. The links and mentions obtained through PR efforts continue to provide value over time, contributing to sustained improvements in search rankings and website traffic.

For the Skincare Academy, the initial boost in rankings and traffic from the press release is just the beginning. As the business continues to grow and receive more mentions and backlinks, its online presence will strengthen, leading to even greater visibility and success.

Practical Tips for Implementing Local PR For Local SEO

Based on our experience with the Skincare Academy, here are some practical tips for leveraging local PR to drive links and improve SEO:

  1. Craft Compelling Press Releases: Ensure your press releases are newsworthy, well-written, and relevant to your target audience.
  2. Target Local Publications: Focus on local news outlets and online publications that are relevant to your business and community.
  3. Build Relationships with Journalists: Establish connections with local journalists and editors to increase the likelihood of your press releases being picked up.
  4. Track and Measure Results: Use tools like Google Search Console and SEMrush to monitor the impact of your PR efforts on search rankings and traffic.

Conclusion: The Transformative Power of Local PR

In conclusion, local PR is a powerful tool for driving links and improving SEO for small businesses. The case study of the Skincare Academy of Florida demonstrates how strategic PR efforts can lead to significant improvements in search rankings and online visibility. By leveraging local PR, businesses can enhance their digital presence, attract more traffic, and ultimately achieve greater success.

At Olympia Marketing, we specialize in helping businesses like the Skincare Academy of Florida unlock the full potential of local PR and SEO. If you're interested in learning more about how local PR can benefit your business, feel free to reach out to us at zach@olympiamarketing.com. Thank you for reading, and stay tuned for our follow-up analysis on the long-term impact of our PR efforts.

title: Why Are My Pages Not on Google? SEO Rapid URL Indexing for Google
content:
SEO Rapid URL Indexing for Google

In the fast-paced world of digital marketing, getting your website content indexed by Google quickly is crucial for staying ahead of the competition. As a seasoned marketing professional, I've encountered numerous challenges when it comes to ensuring that new content is promptly recognized and indexed by Google. In this post, I'll share an effective strategy for achieving rapid URL indexing using a powerful tool called TagParrot. This method can significantly enhance your website's visibility and drive more organic traffic.

Why SEO Rapid URL Indexing Matters

If you're managing a website, you might have noticed that even after publishing new content and setting up Google Search Console, your pages often aren't indexed as quickly as you'd like. This delay can be frustrating, especially when timely indexing is vital for capitalizing on trending topics or new product launches.

Rapid URL indexing ensures that Google quickly recognizes and includes your new pages in search results. This can lead to faster visibility, improved search engine rankings, and ultimately, more traffic to your site. However, manual indexing can be time-consuming and inefficient, which is why leveraging a tool like TagParrot can be a game-changer.

Introducing TagParrot: Your SEO Rapid URL Indexer for Google

TagParrot is a third-party tool designed to automate and accelerate the indexing process for your website. While it requires a nominal investment, the benefits far outweigh the costs, especially if you're managing multiple websites. Here’s how you can set it up and start seeing results almost immediately.

Step-by-Step Guide to Using TagParrot

  1. Set Up Google Search Console:
    • If you haven't already, create a Google Search Console account and add your website. Ensure that your sitemap is properly submitted and communicating with Google.
  2. Integrate TagParrot:
    • Sign up for TagParrot and connect it to your Google Search Console account. This tool will monitor your sitemap and submit new content for indexing as soon as it goes live.
  3. Grant Necessary Permissions:
    • Make sure that TagParrot has the appropriate permissions to access your Search Console data. This will enable it to function effectively and ensure your new pages are indexed promptly.
  4. Monitor and Adjust:
    • Regularly check the status of your indexed pages through TagParrot. The tool will provide insights and analytics on how well your content is being indexed, allowing you to make any necessary adjustments.

Benefits of Using TagParrot for Rapid URL Indexing

  • Time Efficiency:
    • Automating the indexing process saves you significant time, allowing you to focus on other critical aspects of your digital marketing strategy.
  • Improved Visibility:
    • Faster indexing means your content appears in search results sooner, increasing the likelihood of attracting more visitors to your site.
  • Higher Search Engine Rankings:
    • Consistently indexed content can improve your overall SEO performance, leading to better rankings and more organic traffic.

Additional Tips for Boosting Your SEO - Our Top 3 Things We Do...

While TagParrot is an excellent tool for rapid URL indexing, there are other strategies you can implement to enhance your website’s SEO:

  1. Social Media Promotion:
    • Share your new content across your social media platforms to drive initial traffic and signals to Google that your content is valuable and available - in addition to pushing via TagParrot, this usually guarantees indexing in a day.
  2. Backlink Building:
    • Acquire backlinks from reputable sites to boost your domain authority and improve your chances of ranking higher in search results.
    • We use our own blog and others we manage or control to quickly build backlinks to new pillar content or whole new websites, ensuring they're quickly indexed.
  3. Consistent Content Creation:
    • Regularly publish high-quality content to keep your site fresh and engaging for both users and search engines. Even though links are still king - many sites can easily differentiate themselves and ensure better rankings by adding just a little bit of content.

Conclusion

Leveraging a tool like TagParrot for SEO rapid URL indexing can significantly impact your website’s performance on Google. By automating the indexing process, you save time and ensure that your content is quickly recognized and ranked by search engines. Combine this with effective SEO practices, and you'll see a notable improvement in your website's visibility and traffic.

title: 2024 AI & Marketing The Impact of AI Tools on the Marketing Industry
content:
Over the past year, the marketing industry has witnessed a subtle yet significant shift with the integration of AI tools. While the transformative potential of AI has often been heralded as revolutionary, the reality on the ground has been more nuanced. Here, we explore how AI has affected content production, cost structures, customer support, and the future outlook for the marketing sector.

Streamlining Content Production

One of the most noticeable impacts of AI in marketing has been the streamlining of content production. AI tools have proven to be valuable for small tweaks and enhancements in writing blogs, creating website content, and drafting press releases. However, it’s crucial to recognize that these tools are far from replacing human creativity. The role of talented writers has not diminished; instead, it has evolved. Writers now leverage AI for generating prompts and initial drafts, which they then refine and polish. This symbiotic relationship between AI and human ingenuity has led to more efficient workflows and faster turnaround times.

Cost Reduction and Higher Margins

The integration of AI in content creation has resulted in reduced costs for clients and higher profit margins for marketing firms. Automated processes and AI-assisted writing tools have cut down the time and resources required for producing high-quality content. This efficiency translates into cost savings, which can be passed on to clients or retained to boost the bottom line. However, it’s important to note that the extent of these savings is still limited by the need for human oversight and editing.

Limited Impact Beyond Content Production

Despite the buzz around AI, its impact on other aspects of marketing remains relatively modest. The tools we currently use have not yet revolutionized data analysis, campaign management, or strategic decision-making. At best, AI serves as a basic assistant, capable of compiling data, conducting preliminary research, and performing simple informational tasks. The sophistication required to make substantial contributions in these areas is still a work in progress.

AI in Customer Support: A Double-Edged Sword

AI-powered chatbots have become a common feature on websites, offering automated customer support. While these bots can handle basic queries and provide instant responses, their effectiveness is often hampered by limited data access and an inability to comprehend complex issues. As both implementers and users of this technology, we have observed that AI chatbots frequently fall short of delivering the accurate and personalized assistance customers seek. This gap in service quality highlights the current limitations of AI in customer support roles.

The Future of AI in Marketing

Looking ahead, we anticipate a gradual improvement in AI technology. The potential for AI to enhance various marketing functions is undeniable, but it will require significant advancements to realize this potential fully. One area of concern is the reliance on AI for customer support by major tech companies. Historically, companies like Google have struggled with providing effective human support, and the shift to AI-only front-end support could exacerbate these issues. The risk of AI systems defaulting to unhelpful responses or dropping unresolved queries is a genuine concern.

Moreover, there is an emerging worry about the long-term impact of AI on younger generations. As AI tools become more prevalent in educational settings, there is a risk that Gen Z and subsequent generations may become overly dependent on these technologies. This dependence could hinder the development of critical problem-solving skills and independent thinking, posing a challenge for the future workforce.

Conclusion

In its current form, AI in marketing is not the panacea many had hoped for. While it has brought efficiencies and cost savings in content production, its broader impact remains limited. The journey towards more robust and capable AI tools continues, and it is crucial for businesses to navigate this landscape with a balanced perspective. Embracing AI's potential while remaining vigilant about its limitations will be key to harnessing its benefits without compromising on quality and innovation.

As we move forward, a cautious yet optimistic approach will serve the marketing industry well, ensuring that AI complements rather than compromises our core capabilities.
seo_title: AI Marketing in 2025: How Olympia Marketing Drives Results
seo_description: AI in Marketing: Olympia Marketing unveils AI's impact. Learn AI-driven strategies for better marketing results in 2025.

title: Solving DNS Issues with WWW for Domains Hosted on Squarespace
content:
Transitioning a domain from one hosting platform to another can be a complex process, especially when dealing with DNS configurations. Recently, a client encountered a common but tricky issue while moving their domain from Squarespace to WordPress. Although the main domain setup was straightforward, ensuring the "www" prefix worked seamlessly required a bit more finesse. Here's how we resolved the DNS issue and ensured that the client's website was accessible with "www" in the URL.

The Initial Challenge

The client registered their domain through Squarespace and decided to move to WordPress for better flexibility and features. Setting up the primary domain with an A record pointing to the new server's IP address was simple and worked flawlessly. However, the client needed the website to be accessible using "www," as all their marketing materials, including ads, business cards, and stored addresses, prominently featured "www." This necessitated either using "www" directly or implementing a redirect.

The Common Approach

Typically, the solution to this involves creating a CNAME record. In most DNS setups, you can create a CNAME record with "www" in the name field and "@" in the data field. This configuration implies that "www.website.com" points to the main A record for "website.com."

The Issue with Squarespace and GoDaddy

While this approach works with many domain registrars, it failed in this instance due to differences in how GoDaddy and Squarespace handle DNS records. Having primarily worked with GoDaddy for domain needs, the nuance here was initially overlooked. GoDaddy uses the "@" symbol as a shorthand to refer to the main domain name per the A record. However, in this case, using "@" in the CNAME did not resolve the "www" correctly.

The Solution

After considerable troubleshooting, the solution was surprisingly simple: the CNAME record needed to include the full domain name instead of the "@" symbol. Here's the configuration that worked:

  • Record Type: CNAME
  • Name: www
  • Data: website.com (the full main domain name)

By explicitly entering the main domain name in the CNAME record's data field, we ensured that "www.website.com" pointed correctly to the main domain's A record.

Key Takeaways

  1. Understand DNS Variances: Different registrars have their peculiarities in handling DNS records. It is crucial to understand these differences to avoid common pitfalls.
  2. Explicit Entries: When in doubt, use explicit domain names in your DNS entries rather than relying on shorthand symbols or assumptions.
  3. Testing and Validation: Always test DNS changes thoroughly. Use tools like dig or nslookup to verify that DNS changes propagate as expected.
  4. Communication is Key: Keep clients informed of potential issues and solutions to manage expectations and ensure smooth transitions.

Migrating domains between platforms like Squarespace and WordPress can be daunting, especially with DNS complexities. However, understanding the nuances and configurations of your DNS provider can make the process smoother and more efficient. Always remember to validate your settings and test thoroughly to avoid disruptions to your online presence.
seo_title: Squarespace DNS Fix: Olympia Marketing's 2025 Guide
seo_description: Fix Squarespace DNS issues with Olympia Marketing. Learn how to configure your domain settings for optimal website performance.

title: Ultimate Guide Esthetician Marketing Ideas
content:
At Olympia Marketing and our sister marketing agency GlowPro Marketing, we've helped a ton of Esthetician's market their businesses, achieving incredible returns and growing their business. With this guide we sought to create a full breakdown of what we've seen in the past and what we recommend as far as marketing your esthetician business. Some things are incredibly simple and easy, but when done consistently will make your business VERY successful.

Esthetician Marketing Ideas - It Might Not Be Marketing

Before we get to the list of esthetician marketing ideas below we have a slight caveat, having worked with a ton of businesses in the beauty and salon industry, including hundreds of estheticians across the United States here's what we see - more often than not, it's not a marketing issue most esthetician businesses have - it's in fact an administrative/business issue. You can't work 12 hours a day 7 days a week. Instead it's often helpful to understand certain basics of business - that way when you layer on top some form of marketing and begin to scale you'll make MORE money and have to work less AND don't have to work non-stop. But that's for another blog post.

Esthetician Marketing Ideas Outline

  • Key Takeaways

  • Starting from Scratch

    • Building Your Client List

    • Leveraging Personal Networks

    • Offering Initial Discounts

  • Optimizing Google My Business

    • Setting Up Your Profile

    • Gathering Reviews

    • Utilizing Photos and Posts

  • Creating a Website

    • Choosing a Platform

    • Designing User-Friendly Pages

    • Adding Essential Content

  • Using Google Ads

    • Setting Up Ad Campaigns

    • Targeting Local Clients

    • Tracking Ad Performance

  • Implementing SEO Strategies

    • Keyword Research Basics

    • On-Page SEO Tips

    • Building Backlinks

  • Engaging Clients

    • Social Media Interaction

    • Email Marketing Campaigns

    • Hosting Events and Workshops

  • Content Marketing Ideas

    • Blogging Tips

    • Video Content Creation

    • Sharing Client Testimonials

  • Local Community Outreach

    • Partnering with Local Businesses

    • Participating in Community Events

    • Offering Free Workshops

  • Special Promotions and Offers

    • Creating Limited-Time Deals

    • Implementing Loyalty Programs

    • Seasonal Promotions

  • Final Remarks

  • Frequently Asked Questions

    • How can an esthetician benefit from optimizing their Google My Business profile?

    • What are the key elements of creating a successful website for an esthetician?

    • How effective is using Google Ads for esthetician marketing?

    • Why is SEO important for estheticians?

    • How can engaging clients boost an esthetician's business?

    • Can you give examples of content marketing ideas for estheticians?

    • What role does local community outreach play in marketing for estheticians?

    • How do special promotions and offers attract new clients to estheticians?

In the bustling world of beauty and skincare, estheticians face the challenge of shining brighter than their competition. Crafting a marketing strategy that not only attracts but retains clients is no small feat. However, with the right esthetician marketing ideas, turning heads towards your services becomes less of a dream and more of a tangible target. This post dives deep into innovative and effective strategies to elevate your visibility and appeal in the crowded market space. From leveraging social media prowess to mastering the art of word-of-mouth promotion, we unravel how to make your mark and keep your appointment book full. Let's explore how to transform your marketing approach from blending in to standing out.

Key Takeaways

  • Start building your esthetician business's online presence by setting up and optimizing your Google My Business profile. This helps potential clients find you easily.

  • Creating a user-friendly website is crucial. It's your digital storefront and should showcase your services, expertise, and unique selling points.

  • Use Google Ads to target potential clients actively searching for esthetician services. This can be a quick way to increase visibility.

  • Implement SEO strategies to improve your website's ranking in search results, making it easier for clients to discover you.

  • Engage with current and potential clients through social media and email marketing. This builds relationships and encourages loyalty.

  • Get involved in your local community and consider running special promotions to attract new clients and retain existing ones.

Starting from Scratch

Building Your Client List

Developing a strong client base is crucial for success in the esthetician industry. One effective method is creating a referral program. This encourages current clients to talk about your services with others. They might do this by sharing their positive experiences on social media or through direct conversations.

Utilizing social media platforms can also help you reach potential clients in your area. Showcase your work, share skin care tips, and engage with your followers to build interest.

Networking at local events and trade shows is another strategy. Here, you can meet new people who might need your services. Offer them a business card or a sample of your work to turn these encounters into business opportunities.

Leveraging Personal Networks

Your friends and family can be powerful allies in spreading the word about your esthetician services. Don't hesitate to ask them for help. They know you and trust in your abilities, making them perfect advocates for your business.

Offering exclusive discounts to those in your personal network who refer new clients can further incentivize them. This not only broadens your client base but also strengthens relationships with existing ones.

Collaborating with acquaintances who have large followings on social media can significantly boost your visibility. Their endorsement could lead many of their followers to become interested in your services.

Offering Initial Discounts

Attracting new customers often requires an initial incentive. Providing a discount for first-time clients is a tried-and-true method. It makes people more willing to try out your services when they feel like they're getting a good deal.

Creating limited-time offers can generate a sense of urgency, encouraging potential clients to act quickly. This tactic can lead to an immediate increase in bookings.

Bundling services at a reduced rate is another attractive option for new clients. They get more value for their money, and you get the chance to showcase the full range of your skills.

Optimizing Google My Business

Setting Up Your Profile

After laying the groundwork, it's crucial to optimize your online presence. Google My Business (GMB) stands as a powerful tool in this process. First, ensure your profile is comprehensive. This means including up-to-date contact information, a detailed list of services, and your business hours.

Clients look for trustworthy and professional services. By uploading professional photos, you boost your esthetician business's appeal. Photos should cover your workspace, staff, and any products you use or sell.

Don't forget to highlight your qualifications and certifications. These affirm your expertise and build trust with potential clients. They differentiate you from competitors.

Gathering Reviews

Reviews are the backbone of building a reputable online presence. Encourage your satisfied clients to leave positive feedback on both Google and Yelp. This can significantly influence potential clients in their decision-making process.

Following up with clients after their service is a good practice. It shows you value their opinion and encourages them to share their experience online.

Make sure to display these testimonials prominently on your website and social media platforms. They serve as powerful endorsements of your work.

Utilizing Photos and Posts

ial media is a visual platform where before-and-after photos can have a big impact. They provide tangible proof of your skills and the quality of your services. Share these transformations regularly to engage and attract your audience.

Posting consistently keeps your audience engaged and informed about your services or any special offers. It's an effective way to remind them of the value you provide.

Always use high-quality images when showcasing your services or sharing content. This reflects the high standard of your work and professionalism.

Creating a Website

Choosing a Platform

For estheticians looking to expand their online presence, selecting the right platform for their website is crucial. It's important to choose social media platforms where your target audience spends most of their time. Instagram is a top choice for sharing visual content like before-and-after photos or showcasing services. Facebook, on the other hand, excels in building a community through engagement with posts and comments.

Niche platforms should not be overlooked. Pinterest can be a great space for sharing beauty tips and tricks, attracting those interested in skincare and aesthetics. LinkedIn, while more professional, can help establish credibility and connect with other industry professionals.

Designing User-Friendly Pages

A website must be more than just visually appealing; it needs to be user-friendly too. Making sure your site is mobile-friendly is non-negotiable in today’s smartphone-centric world. Visitors should find it easy to navigate through different pages without getting frustrated.

Calls-to-action (CTAs) are another essential feature. They guide visitors towards booking an appointment or contacting you for more information. Optimizing your website’s loading speed can significantly enhance the overall user experience, ensuring potential clients stay on your site longer.

Adding Essential Content

Your website should serve as a comprehensive resource for potential clients. Including detailed descriptions of your services along with pricing information helps set clear expectations. An FAQ section can address common inquiries, saving time for both you and your clients.

A regularly updated blog can also add tremendous value to your website. By providing readers with helpful tips and advice on skincare and beauty, you position yourself as an expert in the field. This not only helps in retaining visitor interest but also boosts your site’s search engine optimization (SEO), making it easier for new clients to discover your services.

Using Google Ads

Setting Up Ad Campaigns

Before diving into esthetician advertising through Google Ads, pinpointing your target audience is crucial. This ensures your ads reach those most likely to book your services. Incorporate eye-catching visuals and compelling copy to grab attention. It's also important to set a realistic budget for your campaigns. This way, you can keep track of your ad spend without overspending.

After creating a website, integrating Google Ads can significantly boost your online presence. By carefully crafting your ads, you can drive more traffic to your site. Remember, the path to a successful campaign lies in detailed planning and execution.

Targeting Local Clients

To effectively capture the interest of local clients, employ geo-targeting in your online ads. This strategy ensures that your advertisements are seen by users in specific locations. Collaborating with local influencers can also widen your reach within the community. Their endorsement can lend credibility to your services.

Attending community events is another excellent way to increase local visibility. It allows you to connect with potential clients face-to-face. These interactions can lead to lasting relationships and a stronger local client base.

Tracking Ad Performance

Utilizing analytics tools is key to understanding the impact of your advertising efforts. They provide insights into how well your campaigns are performing. Based on this data, you might need to tweak your strategies to improve results.

Keep an eye on important metrics like click-through rates and conversions. These indicators help gauge the effectiveness of your ads. If certain ads aren't performing as expected, it may be time for a change. Continuous monitoring and adjusting ensure that your esthetician advertising remains on the right path.

Implementing SEO Strategies

Keyword Research Basics

To attract more clients to your beauty salon, identifying the right keywords is crucial. Potential customers use specific terms when searching for beauty services online. Tools like Google Keyword Planner are invaluable for uncovering these popular search terms. Once identified, integrating these keywords into your website’s content is essential. This approach ensures that your beauty salon marketing strategy aligns with what potential clients are looking for.

Crafting content that seamlessly incorporates these keywords can significantly improve your site's visibility. It's not just about stuffing keywords; it’s about making them a natural part of informative and engaging content.

On-Page SEO Tips

For any beauty salon looking to enhance its online presence, on-page SEO cannot be overlooked. Start by optimizing your title tags and meta descriptions. These elements should be compelling and include relevant keywords to draw in potential clients.

Using header tags like H1 and H2 helps structure your content, making it easier for both readers and search engines to understand the hierarchy and relevance of your information. Also, don't forget about images. Every image on your site should have descriptive alt text. This not only improves accessibility but also gives you an extra opportunity to include important keywords.

Building Backlinks

Backlinks are a cornerstone of any successful beauty salon marketing strategy. They signal to search engines that other websites consider your content valuable enough to link to. One effective technique is reaching out to industry blogs for guest posting opportunities. This not only provides you with a backlink but also exposes your brand to a wider audience.

Collaborating with local businesses for mutual linking can also boost your site’s authority. Submitting your site to relevant directories is a straightforward way to accumulate more backlinks. Remember, the quality of backlinks matters more than quantity.

Engaging Clients

Social Media Interaction

Social media platforms are vital for estheticians aiming to connect with their audience. They should respond quickly to comments and messages. This shows they value their clients' time and opinions. By engaging in conversations and asking questions, they can understand their clients' needs better.

Sharing content created by clients or followers boosts the sense of community. It makes clients feel valued and part of a larger skincare-loving group. This approach not only strengthens existing relationships but also attracts new clients interested in becoming part of this engaged community.

Email Marketing Campaigns

Building an email list is crucial for estheticians looking to expand their reach. They can use their website and social media channels to encourage visitors to sign up. Sending out regular newsletters keeps clients informed about new services, products, and promotions.

Personalization makes emails more effective. By tailoring messages to meet the specific interests and needs of each client, estheticians can increase engagement significantly. This strategy helps maintain interest among existing clients while attracting potential ones by showing that the esthetician values them as individuals.

Hosting Events and Workshops

Events and workshops are excellent ways for skincare professionals to showcase their expertise. Planning skincare workshops educates potential clients about the importance of proper skin care and introduces them to the services offered by the esthetician.

Hosting open house events allows potential clients to experience the esthetician's work environment firsthand. It gives them a taste of the services available. Offering free consultations during these events can be a strong incentive for attendees to book future appointments.

Content Marketing Ideas

Blogging Tips

To captivate your audience, focus on topics that resonate with their interests. This could range from the latest skincare trends to how-to guides on everyday beauty routines. By tailoring your content to meet their needs, you make your blog a go-to resource.

Writing in a conversational tone breaks down complex beauty concepts into easily digestible advice. It makes readers feel like they're getting tips from a friend rather than reading a textbook.

Incorporating images and videos not only enhances the visual appeal of your posts but also supports your explanations and recommendations. Visual aids can significantly boost engagement and understanding, making your blog more effective and enjoyable for your audience.

Video Content Creation

Creating tutorial videos is a powerful way to demonstrate your expertise and show potential clients the value you offer. These tutorials can cover anything from basic skin care routines to advanced esthetic procedures, providing valuable information that viewers can apply in their daily lives.

Client testimonials shared through video format add a layer of authenticity and trust to your brand identity. Seeing real people vouch for your services can significantly influence potential clients' decision-making processes.

Live video sessions offer an unparalleled opportunity to interact with your audience in real-time. Use these sessions to answer questions, provide live demonstrations, or simply chat about beauty-related topics, creating a strong sense of community around your brand.

Sharing Client Testimonials

Featuring testimonials prominently on your website's homepage serves as an immediate endorsement of your services to visitors. It's one of the most effective esthetician marketing ideas because it leverages social proof, encouraging new clients to book appointments.

Sharing client success stories on social media platforms not only highlights the effectiveness of your treatments but also engages your followers by showcasing real-life transformations. This strategy taps into the emotional aspect of beauty marketing space, making it a powerful tool for connection and engagement.

Video testimonials bring a personal touch that text alone cannot convey. They allow prospective clients to hear directly from others about their positive experiences, making this format particularly impactful for building trust and credibility.

Local Community Outreach

Partnering with Local Businesses

Partnering with businesses in your area can boost your reach. Working with local salons and spas allows for cross-promotion. This strategy benefits both parties. You might offer joint packages, combining cosmetic services with their treatments. It creates a comprehensive care package for clients.

Joining local business networking groups is also key. These groups provide opportunities to connect with other businesses. They could become referral sources or partners in promotional efforts.

Participating in Community Events

Community events are perfect for increasing brand visibility. Sponsoring these events puts your name in front of potential clients. It shows you care about the community.

Setting up a booth at community fairs is another effective strategy. It lets you meet people face-to-face. This direct interaction can attract new clients looking for cosmetic services.

Offering free mini-services at these events showcases your skills. It gives people a taste of what you offer. This can lead to them booking full treatments later on.

Offering Free Workshops

Free workshops are a powerful tool for attracting potential clients. Hosting skincare workshops educates the community about proper skin care. It positions you as an expert in your field.

These workshops also provide valuable information to attendees. They see firsthand the quality of care and treatment you provide. Collecting contact information from attendees allows for follow-up marketing. This can convert attendees into clients.

Special Promotions and Offers

Creating Limited-Time Deals

Limited-time deals can spark interest and generate quick sales. During holidays like Christmas or Valentine’s Day, estheticians can offer special packages on beauty services. This not only aligns with the festive mood but also encourages clients to indulge in treatments they might not usually consider.

Creating a sense of urgency is crucial. Flash sales, for instance, can create a buzz, prompting immediate action from potential clients. They might fear missing out on a great deal, so they book appointments faster. This strategy works well for both skincare services and professional skincare products.

Implementing Loyalty Programs

Loyalty programs are a win-win. They reward repeat clients, making them feel valued and encouraging further bookings. A points-based system is effective here. Clients could earn points for every dollar spent on treatments or products, redeemable against future purchases.

Rewards for loyal customers can be varied: from discounts on their next visit to freebies or exclusive access to new treatments. It's important to promote these programs effectively. Using email marketing and social media can help spread the word, turning one-time clients into long-term patrons.

Seasonal Promotions

Aligning promotions with the seasons can significantly boost interest in skincare services. For summer, estheticians might focus on sun protection and hydration treatments, offering packages that cater specifically to these needs. Winter, on the other hand, could focus on moisturizing treatments to combat dry skin.

Holiday-themed packages create an opportunity for gift-giving or self-care during festive periods. These can include special offers on professional skincare products or bundled treatments at a discounted rate.

Marketing campaigns should leverage seasonal trends, using them to craft timely and appealing offers. This approach keeps the service offerings fresh and relevant throughout the year.

Final Remarks

Boosting your esthetician business isn't just about offering top-notch services; it's also about how effectively you market those services. From starting with the basics to engaging in local community outreach, each strategy you've learned here aims to elevate your visibility and attract more clients. Optimizing your Google My Business profile, creating a compelling website, leveraging Google Ads, and implementing SEO strategies are foundational steps. Engaging with clients directly, content marketing, and running special promotions are the layers that add depth to your marketing efforts. Your journey doesn't stop at applying these ideas; it's about continuously adapting and finding what resonates with your audience.

Now's the time to put these ideas into action. Start small, measure your results, and adjust as necessary. Remember, the most successful marketing strategy is one that evolves with your business and your clients' needs. Ready to boost your esthetician business? Dive in and let these strategies work wonders for you.

Frequently Asked Questions

How can an esthetician benefit from optimizing their Google My Business profile?

Optimizing your Google My Business profile increases visibility on local searches, making it easier for potential clients to find and choose your services over competitors.

What are the key elements of creating a successful website for an esthetician?

A successful website should be user-friendly, mobile-optimized, visually appealing, and include clear service descriptions, pricing, and easy booking options to attract and retain clients.

How effective is using Google Ads for esthetician marketing?

Google Ads can be highly effective in targeting potential clients actively searching for esthetic services, offering quick visibility and driving traffic to your website or booking page.

Why is SEO important for estheticians?

Implementing SEO strategies improves your website's ranking in search results, leading to increased organic traffic and higher chances of attracting new clients looking for esthetic services.

How can engaging clients boost an esthetician's business?

Engaging with clients through personalized communication, feedback requests, and loyalty programs builds strong relationships, encouraging repeat business and referrals.

Can you give examples of content marketing ideas for estheticians?

Content marketing ideas include creating skincare tips blogs, how-to videos on skin health, before-and-after client testimonials, and sharing expert advice on social media platforms to establish authority and connect with your audience.

What role does local community outreach play in marketing for estheticians?

Participating in local events, collaborating with other businesses, and sponsoring community activities raise your profile, build local connections, and directly market your services to potential clients in your area.

How do special promotions and offers attract new clients to estheticians?

Special promotions and offers incentivize new clients to try your services at a reduced risk, providing an opportunity to impress them with your expertise and convert them into regular customers.
